covid19_wildtype.description: Pre-Omicron SARS-CoV-2 lineage with moderate reproduction number and multi-day incubation.
covid19_wildtype.provenance: Matches GARLAND SEIRConfig defaults; beta and compartment rates calibrated so estimate_r0() returns reference R0 ~2.5.
references: Li et al., Early transmission dynamics in Wuhan (Science, 2020)

covid19_omicron.description: Highly transmissible Omicron variant with shorter generation interval than wild-type.
covid19_omicron.provenance: Incubation ~3 days and infectious ~7 days; beta calibrated to reference R0 ~8.0 using the wild-type contact-scaling factor.
references: Lyngse et al., Omicron transmission in Danish households (Eurosurveillance, 2022)

influenza_seasonal.description: Endemic respiratory influenza with lower R0 and shorter infectious period than SARS-CoV-2.
influenza_seasonal.provenance: Incubation ~2 days and infectious ~5 days; beta calibrated to reference R0 ~1.3 using the wild-type contact-scaling factor.
references: Carrat et al., Time lines of infection and disease in human influenza (AJE, 2008)

measles.description: Airborne childhood disease with very high reproduction number and long incubation.
measles.provenance: Incubation ~10 days and infectious ~8 days; beta calibrated to reference R0 ~15.0 using the wild-type contact-scaling factor.
references: Anderson & May, Infectious Diseases of Humans (1991)

rsv.description: Pediatric and seasonal respiratory virus with moderate transmissibility.
rsv.provenance: Incubation ~4 days and infectious ~7 days; beta calibrated to reference R0 ~3.0.
references: Hall et al., Respiratory syncytial virus-associated hospitalizations (NEJM, 2009)

norovirus.description: Acute gastroenteritis pathogen with short incubation and short shedding period; included for contrast with respiratory benchmarks.
norovirus.provenance: Incubation ~1.5 days and infectious ~3 days; beta calibrated to reference R0 ~2.0. Contact radius widened to reflect fomite and close-contact spread in shared settings.
references: Atmar et al., Norwalk virus shedding after experimental human infection (JID, 2008)
